lessons.title: First Win: Pitch Lock + Best Take
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Learn the loop. Calm pitch lock. Confidence-building wins.

### Objectives
- Complete the full loop end-to-end
- Hold a note inside the lane for 2s
- Save one best take you’re proud of

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Gentle hum (60–90s) — soft and steady
- NG resonance (60s) — relaxed throat
- Tiny glide (30–60s) — no strain

### Core drills
- **Warmup hum** — 30–45s. Focus on steady airflow.
- **Match & lock** — 5 reps. Lock quickly, then stay still for ~2s.
- **Sustain steady** — 4 reps. Keep it smooth; micro-correct only.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- 2 reps with overlay faded after 1s (listen and rely on feel).

### Close
- Replay the best rep (waveform) and save as Best Take.
- Next: Repeatability day.
keyPoints: Save a best take based on cleanliness, not just score.
doThis: If you miss, reset and go again.
coachScript: Today is about a *win*. Start gentle. Lock the note, then keep it still.
If you drift, do a tiny adjustment and return to calm.
After each rep, listen once. Save the cleanest take.

meta.focus: Learn the loop. Calm pitch lock. Confidence-building wins.

lessons.title: Repeatability: Stable Holds (Day 2)
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Build consistency: repeat the same success with less effort.

### Objectives
- Get 3+ stable reps
- Reduce ‘wobble’ and overshooting
- Save a best take that feels easier than Day 1

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum + NG (2 min)
- Small slide (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Warmup match** — 3 reps. Match pitch gently.
- **Match & lock** — 5 reps. Aim for quicker lock than yesterday.
- **Sustain steady** — 4 reps. Focus on steadiness (not louder).

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- Do 1 rep with bandwidth-only mindset: only correct when clearly outside the lane.

### Close
- Save today’s best take.
- Note: ‘Did this feel easier?’ (yes/no).
keyPoints: Make the correction smaller than you think.
doThis: Repeat the same setup each rep (posture, breath).
Listen once, then do the next rep.
coachScript: We’re building repeatability. Same setup every time.
Aim for ‘easy wins’ rather than ‘big wins’.

meta.focus: Build consistency: repeat the same success with less effort.

lessons.title: Stability Ladder W2D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Longer holds and cleaner endings. Make stability automatic.

### Objectives
- Increase hold time without tension
- End cleanly
- Save best take with smoother tail

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- NG (1 min)
- Easy sustain (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Sustain steady** — 5 reps. 2–3s holds.
- **Breath phrase** — 3 reps. Smooth airflow for 4s.
- **Vibrato control** — 3 reps. Keep wobble small and controlled.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- 1 rep with overlay faded after 1.2s. Focus on feel + breath.

### Close
- Save best take. Tomorrow: stability under small variation.
keyPoints: Longer holds require less push, not more.
Clean endings are part of ‘pro’ singing.
doThis: Release the end cleanly—don’t ‘search’ for pitch on the way out.
coachScript: Longer holds feel calmer. If you tense, shorten and rebuild.
We care about the end as much as the middle.

meta.focus: Longer holds and cleaner endings. Make stability automatic.

lessons.title: 2-Note Steps W3D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Clean transitions between two notes (up/down).

### Objectives
- Move between notes without sliding too far
- Stabilize quickly after the change

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- Slide small (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Slide warmup** — 3 reps. Small controlled glide.
- **Interval step** — 5 reps. Hit the second note cleanly.
- **Stabilize** — 3 reps. Hold steady after movement.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- Do 2 reps with faded overlay; judge success by listening to playback.

### Close
- Save the cleanest transition take.
- Next: add stability after transition.
keyPoints: Transition is quick; stability is the goal.
coachScript: Hit the change, then go still. Playback will tell you the truth.

meta.focus: Clean transitions between two notes (up/down).

lessons.title: Onset + Offset Control W4D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Clean starts and endings; reduce scoops and pitch tails.

### Objectives
- Start on pitch quickly
- End without drift
- Improve median consistency over reps

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- NG (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Resonance forward** — 4 reps. Forward placement.
- **Slide down control** — 4 reps. Control descent.
- **Melody echo** — 3 reps. Short phrase accuracy.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- 1 no-overlay rep (post summary only).

### Close
- Save best take and compare to last week’s best take.
keyPoints: A clean onset is gentle and precise.
A clean offset releases, not collapses.
doThis: Release the end as if ‘placing’ silence.
avoidThis: Letting pitch fall at the end.
coachScript: Treat starts and ends like punctuation. Clean edges sound pro.

meta.focus: Clean starts and endings; reduce scoops and pitch tails.

meta.focus: Clean starts and endings; reduce scoops and pitch tails.

lessons.title: Agility Runs + Echo Phrases W5D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Agility and short melodic accuracy under control.

### Objectives
- Move smoothly without losing pitch center
- Keep rhythm and pitch stable

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- Small pentatonic run (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Agility run** — 4 reps. Smooth and even.
- **Melody echo** — 5 reps. Copy the phrase precisely.
- **Stability hold** — 2 reps. Reset steadiness.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- 2 reps with faded overlay; focus on listening for accuracy.

### Close
- Save best take. Next: vary vowel or range (controlled variation).
coachScript: Clean agility is relaxed. Slow is professional.

meta.focus: Agility and short melodic accuracy under control.

lessons.title: Variation & Robustness W6D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Same skills under controlled variation (range/vowel/noise).

### Objectives
- Hold performance under variation
- Stay calm when confidence dips

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- Breath phrase (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Breath phrase** — 3 reps. Stable airflow.
- **Slide up** — 4 reps. Controlled ascent.
- **Song phrase** — 2 reps. Simple musical transfer.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- 1 rep with bandwidth-only overlay; 1 rep with overlay off.

### Close
- Save a best take that is ‘clean under variation’.
keyPoints: Variation is training, not a test.
doThis: If tracking is noisy, focus on breath + steady vowel.
coachScript: If it gets messy, return to calm breath and a steady tone. That’s the pro move.

meta.focus: Same skills under controlled variation (range/vowel/noise).

lessons.title: Transfer & Independence W7D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Reduce reliance on visual overlay; build performance resilience.

### Objectives
- Perform with minimal live feedback
- Use post summary to correct one thing

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- Resonance (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Match & lock** — 4 reps. Minimal corrections.
- **Melody echo** — 4 reps. Rely on ear.
- **Song phrase** — 2 reps. Performance feel.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- All reps are faded or off. After each, pick ONE correction and redo.

### Close
- Save best take. Write one sentence: ‘My one correction today was…’
doThis: Keep rep count low and quality high.
avoidThis: Trying to fix everything at once.
coachScript: Today you train independence. Trust your ear; use the summary as your coach.

meta.focus: Reduce reliance on visual overlay; build performance resilience.

lessons.title: Graduation Set W8D1
lessons.body:
**Focus:** Pro-feel loop: choose best take, consistency under pressure, next program.

### Objectives
- Demonstrate stable pitch + transitions
- Save best take under limited attempts
- Plan next focus

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Hum (1 min)
- Breath phrase (1 min)

### Core drills
- **Interval step** — 3 reps. Clean transitions.
- **Vibrato control** — 2 reps. Stable hold.
- **Song phrase** — 2 reps. Musical transfer.

### Transfer (reduced feedback)
- Limit to 3 total attempts. Choose best take and commit.

### Close
- Save best take. Review week-to-week progress (best take history).
- Next: pick a specialization path (stability / agility / songs).
keyPoints: Choose and commit to the best take.
coachScript: This is your graduation set. You’re training confidence and decision-making.

meta.focus: Pro-feel loop: choose best take, consistency under pressure, next program.
